title: AUC-Optimized Super Learner Instead Of Grid-Blended Ranks
summary: The baseline still chooses blends mostly by hand-crafted grids and rank heuristics.
rationale: The baseline still chooses blends mostly by hand-crafted grids and rank heuristics. Super Learner and AUC-maximizing metalearning are explicitly designed to learn convex combinations that maximize cross-validated ROC AUC, which is the exact leaderboard metric.
implementation_hint: Create fold-safe base learners including current XGB, CatBoost, ranking expert, and choice expert. Train a non-negative meta-learner on OOF predictions plus a few meta-features such as adversarial-shift score, query size, wet/dry flag, and ambiguity margin. Optimize the meta-learner for AUC, not logloss. Keep final output as OOF-trained stacking only.
expected_effect: Most plausible short-path gain for this codebase because it upgrades the already-successful ensemble stage rather than replacing the core models.
risk: Meta-overfitting is the main danger; all stacker inputs and any calibration must be strictly OOF and preferably checked on chronological splits.
